---
title: "Geocomputation with R"
subtitle: "useR! 2026 — hands-on tutorial"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

Welcome to the **Geocomputation with R** tutorial at useR! 2026.
The tutorial is presented by Jannes Muenchow, Robin Lovelace and Jakub Nowosad,
authors of the book [*Geocomputation with R*](https://r.geocompx.org/) (2nd edition).

## Slides

- [1 · Introduction](slides/01-intro.qmd)
- [2 · Coordinate reference systems](slides/02-crs.qmd)
- [3 · Vector data with **sf**](slides/03-vector.qmd)
- [4 · Raster data with **terra**](slides/04-raster.qmd)
- 4 · Visualization with **tmap** *(coming soon)*
- [5 · Bonus: Bridges to GIS with **qgisprocess**](slides/05-qgisprocess.qmd) *(needs the `qgis` Docker image)*

## Exercise solutions

- [`solutions/02-crs-solution.R`](solutions/02-crs-solution.R)
- [`solutions/03-vector-solution.R`](solutions/03-vector-solution.R)
- [`solutions/04-raster-solution.R`](solutions/04-raster-solution.R)

## Setup

A pre-built Docker image containing R, RStudio and all required packages will be
provided. Alternatively, install the packages into a local R (≥ 4.3) session:

```r
install.packages(c("sf", "terra", "tmap", "spData",
                   "dplyr", "leaflet", "mapview"))
```
